user_query:
Set up my Q2 home maintenance plan using the local maintenance tool. Read everything under input/ and then:

1) Add inventory entries for every row in input/inventory.csv (include name, location, model, serial, purchase_date in the entry text).
2) Add checklist entries from input/checklist.md.
3) For each task in input/plan.yaml, create schedule entries with the exact date(s) and details ("<date> — <item>: <task>") and also create matching remind entries (use the reminder instructions in the YAML, e.g., "<reminder rule> for <date>").
4) Add cost entries for each line in input/costs.csv ("<date> — <item> (<category>): $<cost>").
5) Save the output of "maintenance status" to output/status.txt and "maintenance stats" to output/stats.txt.
6) Run "maintenance export json" and "maintenance export csv" and copy the resulting files to output/export.json and output/export.csv.
7) Run "maintenance search HVAC" and "maintenance search water" and save their outputs to output/search_hvac.txt and output/search_water.txt.
8) Create output/plan_summary.md that summarizes: Inventory, Schedule, Reminders, Checklist, and Costs. Include the quarter string from input/plan.yaml and a single line "Total Quarterly Cost: $391.50" computed from input/costs.csv. Make it human-readable with those exact section headings.
9) Create output/notifications.yaml listing each reminder with its date(s) in a simple YAML structure (top-level key: reminders).
